data.title: Dow Jones futures dropped, Bitcoin fell below $70,000, Google's AI spending surprises investors amid divided stock market.
data.description: Google reported robust earnings, however, their spending on artificial intelligence was surprising. Bitcoin's value is on a downward trend, dropping below $70,000, while the stock market is experiencing significant division. Dow Jones futures are also falling in response to these developments.

data.title: Kyivstar prices secondary offering of 12.5M shares by VEON at $10.50/share, with additional option for 1.87M shares.
data.description: In New York on January 29, 2026, VEON Ltd. (Nasdaq: VEON), a global digital operator, highlighted that Kyivstar Group Ltd. (Nasdaq: KYIV; KYIVW), the top digital operator in Ukraine, disclosed the pricing details for a public offering. The offering consists of 12,100,000 common shares owned by VEON Amsterdam B.V. and 400,000 common shares held by other selling shareholders, valued at USD 10.50 per share. Additionally, the Selling Shareholders granted underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 1,875,000 additional common shares at the same price, excluding underwriting discounts and commissions.

data.title: Bitcoin holders show resilience as bear floor 20% lower, trading in 2024 demand zone, but data points to potential new lows.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently trading within a narrow demand zone established in 2024. However, historical data from past bear markets indicates that this trend is likely to surpass the established channel, resulting in potential new lows for the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Mitsubishi Logisnext Americas reveals winners of UniCarriers Forklifts' Premier Club Awards for 2025.
data.description: In Marengo, Illinois, on February 19, 2026, Mitsubishi Logisnext Americas announced the winners of the Premier Club Awards for 2025. The awards, presented by the exclusive manufacturer and distributor of UniCarriers® Forklifts in North, Central, and South America, recognize the top performers within the company.

data.title: Inventor from Spring Lake, N.C. develops HOME BUTLER SAFETY BUTLER, a door hinge pin for added convenience & security.
data.description: A resident of Spring Lake, N.C. mentioned his motivation behind creating a new product to assist homeowners in hanging various items momentarily. The innovative invention, named HOME BUTLER SAFETY BUTLER, aims to offer a convenient solution for individuals looking to hang items like purses, bags, dry cleaning, or keys. The inventor unveiled the product with the intention of providing added ease to homeowners in temporarily storing their belongings.

data.title: Republic Power Group Limited is conducting a 1-for-20 reverse share split of its Class A and Class B ordinary shares.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, in Singapore, Republic Power Group Limited (NASDAQ: RPGL) revealed plans for a reverse share split of its Class A and Class B ordinary shares. This includes a combined par value of $0.000625 per share for both categories.

data.title: Stocks dip as Trump hints at Iran strike, oil hits 7-month high at $66.65/barrel. S&P 500 down 0.3%, Nasdaq and Dow down 0.5%. Energy sector gains 0.8%, Occidental Petroleum (NYSA: OXY) up 9% and Deere & Company (NYSA: DE) up 12.6%.
data.description: U.S. stocks dipped as President Trump hinted at a possible military intervention in Iran, pushing crude prices up for a second day. West Texas Intermediate futures surged 2.5% to $66.65 per barrel, the highest since August 2025. The S&P 500 fell by 0.3%, Nasdaq 100 and Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped about 0.5%. Energy stocks rose 0.8% while financials lagged. Occidental Petroleum Corp. shares surged 9%, while Deere & Company jumped 12.6%, leading to a 27% month-to-date gain.

data.title: Fujifilm to showcase advanced lithography research at SPIE Conference, aiming to double semiconductor revenue to $3.26B by 2030.
data.description: In Tokyo, FUJIFILM Corporation is set to showcase its recent research discoveries at SPIE Advanced Lithography + Patterning 2026 from February 22-26, 2026, in San Jose, California. The event, managed by SPIE, the worldwide group for optics and photonics, serves as a platform for unveiling the company's vision. As FUJIFILM pursues a growth strategy to augment revenue in the semiconductor materials sector, aiming to reach 500 billion yen (equivalent to 3.26 billion USD) by FY2030 in contrast to FY2024, the focus lies on this key objective to enhance its market presence.

data.title: The Pokémon Company International buys Excell Brands, a top U.S. distributor and service provider.
data.description: Bellevue, Washington - The Pokémon Company International has announced its acquisition of Excell Brands, a prominent U.S. distributor and provider of category management services. This move signifies a strategic development in the company's expansion efforts, involving an undisclosed financial transaction as part of the acquisition deal. This acquisition is expected to enhance The Pokémon Company International's market presence and streamline its distribution operations in the U.S.

data.title: Velocys achieves over 30% cost reduction in microFTL technology deployment, advancing 2 flagship FT SAF projects.
data.description: Houston and Oxford, England - Velocys revealed on February 19, 2026, that they have successfully enhanced manufacturing and delivery processes resulting in a significant reduction of over 30% in the total investment cost for their microFTL™ technology. These improvements were validated during the design phase, showcasing the company's commitment to cost-effectiveness.

data.title: Broadcom launches game-changing BroadPeak radio digital front-end SoC for 5G/6G, offering up to 40% power reduction and advanced tech.
data.description: Broadcom has launched the BroadPeak radio digital front-end SoC device aimed at enhancing 5G and 6G wireless infrastructure. This is the first digital radio front-end to meet upcoming 5G Advanced requirements in the n104 band and future 6G upper mid-band needs. The device promises up to a 40% power reduction compared to existing solutions and features state-of-the-art 5nm CMOS technology. Broadcom has started shipping samples of the BCM85021 to early access customers. This news caused Broadcom's shares to rise, contrasting the broader market's decline with major indices like S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing lower. The device positions Broadcom as a key player in the upcoming 5G Advanced and 6G standards, catering to the increasing demand for enhanced mobile network capabilities.
